debug_info.goal:
V : Type u_1
Ω : Type u_2
m : MeasurableSpace Ω
G H : Ω → SimpleGraph V
p q : ↑unitInterval
P : Measure Ω
inst✝ : Countable V
hG : @SimpleGraph.IsBinomialRandom V Ω m G p P
hY : @SimpleGraph.IsBinomialRandom V Ω m H q P
⊢ Measure.map (fun ω => G ω ⊔ H ω) P =
    @SimpleGraph.binomialRandom V (unitInterval.symm (unitInterval.symm p * unitInterval.symm q))

by
    have h₁ : edgeSet '' {G ω ⊔ H ω | ω : Ω} = edgeSet '' {G ω ⊔ H ω | ω : Ω} := rfl
    have h₂ : Measure.map (fun ω => G ω ⊔ H ω) P = Measure.map (fun ω => G ω ⊔ H ω) P := rfl
    simp_all [IsBinomialRandom, binomialRandom]
    <;>
    congr 1 <;>
    ext <;>
    simp_all [edgeSet]
    <;>
    aesop
  aemeasurable := by
    have h₃ : Measurable (fun ω => G ω ⊔ H ω) := by
      -- Prove that the union of two measurable simple graphs is measurable.
      have h₄ : Measurable G := by
        -- Since G is binomial random, it is measurable.
        exact hG.1
      have h₅ : Measurable H := by
        -- Since H is binomial random, it is measurable.
        exact hY.1
      -- The union of two measurable functions is measurable.
      exact h₄.sup h₅
    -- The union map is measurable, hence the distribution is aemeasurable.
    exact h₃.aemeasurable
